1527: // resolveErrors is schedule by scheduleErrorResolver() and runs periodically.
1528: // It resolves up to maxSyncErrors discrepancies between cache and BPF map in
1529: // the kernel.
1530: func (m *Map) resolveErrors(ctx context.Context) error {
1531: 	started := time.Now()
1532: 
1533: 	m.lock.Lock()
1534: 	defer m.lock.Unlock()
1535: 
1536: 	if m.cache == nil {
1537: 		return nil
1538: 	}
1539: 
1540: 	if !m.outstandingErrors {
1541: 		return nil
1542: 	}
1543: 
1544: 	outstanding := 0
1545: 	for _, e := range m.cache {
1546: 		switch e.DesiredAction {
1547: 		case Insert, Delete:
1548: 			outstanding++
1549: 		}
1550: 	}
1551: 
1552: 	// Errors appear to have already been resolved. This can happen if a subsequent
1553: 	// Update/Delete operation acting on the same key succeeded.
1554: 	if outstanding == 0 {
1555: 		m.outstandingErrors = false
1556: 		return nil
1557: 	}
1558: 
1559: 	if err := m.open(); err != nil {
1560: 		return err
1561: 	}
1562: 
1563: 	m.Logger.Debug(
1564: 		"Starting periodic BPF map error resolver",
1565: 		logfields.Remaining, outstanding,
1566: 	)
1567: 
1568: 	resolved := 0
1569: 	scanned := 0
1570: 	nerr := 0
1571: 	for k, e := range m.cache {
1572: 		scanned++
1573: 
1574: 		switch e.DesiredAction {
1575: 		case OK:
1576: 		case Insert:
1577: 			// Call into ebpf-go's Map.Update() directly, don't go through the cache.
1578: 			err := m.m.Update(e.Key, e.Value, ebpf.UpdateAny)
1579: 			if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
1580: 				metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpUpdate, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
1581: 			}
1582: 			if err == nil {
1583: 				e.DesiredAction = OK
1584: 				e.LastError = nil
1585: 				resolved++
1586: 				outstanding--
1587: 			} else {
1588: 				e.LastError = err
1589: 				nerr++
1590: 			}
1591: 			m.cache[k] = e
1592: 			m.addToEventsLocked(MapUpdate, *e)
1593: 		case Delete:
1594: 			// Holding lock, issue direct delete on map.
1595: 			err := m.m.Delete(e.Key)
1596: 			if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
1597: 				metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpDelete, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
1598: 			}
1599: 			if err == nil || errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) {
1600: 				delete(m.cache, k)
1601: 				resolved++
1602: 				outstanding--
1603: 			} else {
1604: 				e.LastError = err
1605: 				nerr++
1606: 				m.cache[k] = e
1607: 			}
1608: 
1609: 			m.addToEventsLocked(MapDelete, *e)
1610: 		}
1611: 
1612: 		// bail out if maximum errors are reached to relax the map lock
1613: 		if nerr > maxSyncErrors {
1614: 			break
1615: 		}
1616: 	}
1617: 
1618: 	m.updatePressureMetric()
1619: 
1620: 	m.Logger.Debug(
1621: 		"BPF map error resolver completed",
1622: 		logfields.Remaining, outstanding,
1623: 		logfields.Resolved, resolved,
1624: 		logfields.Scanned, scanned,
1625: 		logfields.Duration, time.Since(started),
1626: 	)
1627: 
1628: 	m.outstandingErrors = outstanding > 0
1629: 	if m.outstandingErrors {
1630: 		return fmt.Errorf("%d map sync errors", outstanding)
1631: 	}
1632: 
1633: 	return nil
1634: }

89: func (m *Map) initEventsBuffer(maxSize int, eventsTTL time.Duration) {
90: 	b := &eventsBuffer{
91: 		logger:   m.Logger,
92: 		buffer:   container.NewRingBuffer(maxSize),
93: 		eventTTL: eventsTTL,
94: 	}
95: 	if b.eventTTL > 0 {
96: 		m.Logger.Debug("starting bpf map event buffer GC controller")
97: 		mapControllers.UpdateController(
98: 			fmt.Sprintf("bpf-event-buffer-gc-%s", m.name),
99: 			controller.ControllerParams{
100: 				Group: bpfEventBufferGCControllerGroup,
101: 				DoFunc: func(_ context.Context) error {
102: 					m.Logger.Debug(
103: 						"clearing bpf map events older than TTL",
104: 						logfields.TTL, b.eventTTL,
105: 					)
106: 					b.buffer.Compact(func(e any) bool {
107: 						event, ok := e.(*Event)
108: 						if !ok {
109: 							m.Logger.Error("Failed to compact the event buffer", logfields.Error, wrongObjTypeErr(e))
110: 							return false
111: 						}
112: 						return time.Since(event.Timestamp) < b.eventTTL
113: 					})
114: 					return nil
115: 				},
116: 				RunInterval: b.eventTTL,
117: 			},
118: 		)
119: 	}
120: 	m.events = b
121: }

123: // eventsBuffer stores a buffer of events for auditing and debugging
124: // purposes.
125: type eventsBuffer struct {
126: 	logger        *slog.Logger
127: 	buffer        *container.RingBuffer
128: 	eventTTL      time.Duration
129: 	subsLock      lock.RWMutex
130: 	subscriptions []*Handle
131: }
132: 
133: // Handle allows for handling event streams safely outside of this package.
134: // The key design consideration for event streaming is that it is non-blocking.
135: // The eventsBuffer takes care of closing handles when their consumer is not reading
136: // off the buffer (or is not reading off it fast enough).
137: type Handle struct {
138: 	c      chan *Event
139: 	closed atomic.Bool
140: 	closer *sync.Once
141: 	err    error
142: }
143: 
144: // Returns read only channel for Handle subscription events. Channel should be closed with
145: // handle.Close() function.
146: func (h *Handle) C() <-chan *Event {
147: 	return h.c // return read only channel to prevent closing outside of Close(...).
148: }
149: 
150: // Close allows for safaley closing of a handle.
151: func (h *Handle) Close() {
152: 	h.close(nil)
153: }
154: 
155: func (h *Handle) close(err error) {
156: 	h.closer.Do(func() {
157: 		close(h.c)
158: 		h.err = err
159: 		h.closed.Store(true)
160: 	})
161: }
162: 
163: func (h *Handle) isClosed() bool {
164: 	return h.closed.Load()
165: }
166: 
167: func (h *Handle) isFull() bool {
168: 	return len(h.c) >= cap(h.c)
169: }
170: 
171: // This configures how big buffers are for channels used for streaming events from
172: // eventsBuffer.
173: //
174: // To prevent blocking bpf.Map operations, subscribed events are buffered per client handle.
175: // How fast subscribers will need to proceess events will depend on the event throughput.
176: // In this case, our throughput will be expected to be not above 100 events a second.
177: // Therefore the consumer will have 10ms to process each event. The channel is also
178: // given a constant buffer size in the case where events arrive at once (i.e. all 100 events
179: // arriving at the top of the second).
180: //
181: // NOTE: Although using timers/timed-contexts seems like an obvious choice for this use case,
182: // the timer.After implementation actually uses a large amount of memory. To reduce memory spikes
183: // in high throughput cases, we instead just use a sufficiently buffered channel.
184: const (
185: 	eventSubChanBufferSize = 32
186: 	maxConcurrentEventSubs = 32
187: )
188: 
189: func (eb *eventsBuffer) hasSubCapacity() bool {
190: 	eb.subsLock.RLock()
191: 	defer eb.subsLock.RUnlock()
192: 	return len(eb.subscriptions) <= maxConcurrentEventSubs
193: }
194: 
195: func (eb *eventsBuffer) dumpAndSubscribe(callback EventCallbackFunc, follow bool) (*Handle, error) {
196: 	if follow && !eb.hasSubCapacity() {
197: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exceeded max number of concurrent map event subscriptions %d", maxConcurrentEventSubs)
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	if callback != nil {
201: 		eb.dumpWithCallback(callback)
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	if !follow {
205: 		return nil, nil
206: 	}
207: 
208: 	h := &Handle{
209: 		c:      make(chan *Event, eventSubChanBufferSize),
210: 		closer: &sync.Once{},
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	eb.subsLock.Lock()
214: 	defer eb.subsLock.Unlock()
215: 	eb.subscriptions = append(eb.subscriptions, h)
216: 	return h, nil
217: }
218: 
219: // DumpAndSubscribe dumps existing buffer, if callback is not nil. Followed by creating a
220: // subscription to the maps events buffer and returning the handle.
221: // These actions are done together so as to prevent possible missed events between the handoff
222: // of the callback and sub handle creation.
223: func (m *Map) DumpAndSubscribe(callback EventCallbackFunc, follow bool) (*Handle, error) {
224: 	// note: we have to hold rlock for the duration of this to prevent missed events between dump and sub.
225: 	// dumpAndSubscribe maintains its own write-lock for updating subscribers.
226: 	m.lock.RLock()
227: 	defer m.lock.RUnlock()
228: 	if !m.eventsBufferEnabled {
229: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("map events not enabled for map %q", m.name)
230: 	}
231: 	return m.events.dumpAndSubscribe(callback, follow)
232: }
233: 
234: func (m *Map) IsEventsEnabled() bool {
235: 	return m.eventsBufferEnabled
236: }
237: 
238: func (eb *eventsBuffer) add(e *Event) {
239: 	eb.buffer.Add(e)
240: 	var activeSubs []*Handle
241: 	activeSubsLock := &lock.Mutex{}
242: 	wg := &sync.WaitGroup{}
243: 	for i, sub := range eb.subscriptions {
244: 		if sub.isClosed() { // sub will be removed.
245: 			continue
246: 		}
247: 		wg.Add(1)
248: 		go func(sub *Handle, i int) {
249: 			defer wg.Done()
250: 			if sub.isFull() {
251: 				err := fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting to send sub map event")
252: 				eb.logger.Warn(
253: 					"subscription channel buffer was full, closing subscription",
254: 					logfields.Error, err,
255: 					logfields.SubscriptionID, i,
256: 				)
257: 				sub.close(err)
258: 			} else {
259: 				sub.c <- e
260: 				activeSubsLock.Lock()
261: 				activeSubs = append(activeSubs, sub)
262: 				activeSubsLock.Unlock()
263: 			}
264: 		}(sub, i)
265: 	}
266: 	wg.Wait()
267: 	eb.subsLock.Lock()
268: 	defer eb.subsLock.Unlock()
269: 	eb.subscriptions = activeSubs
270: }
271: 
272: func wrongObjTypeErr(i any) error {
273: 	return fmt.Errorf("BUG: wrong object type in event ring buffer: %T", i)
274: }
275: 
276: func (eb *eventsBuffer) eventIsValid(e any) bool {
277: 	event, ok := e.(*Event)
278: 	if !ok {
279: 		eb.logger.Error("Could not dump contents of events buffer", logfields.Error, wrongObjTypeErr(e))
280: 		return false
281: 	}
282: 	return eb.eventTTL == 0 || time.Since(event.Timestamp) <= eb.eventTTL
283: }
284: 
285: // EventCallbackFunc is used to dump events from a event buffer.
286: type EventCallbackFunc func(*Event)
287: 
288: func (eb *eventsBuffer) dumpWithCallback(callback EventCallbackFunc) {
289: 	eb.buffer.IterateValid(eb.eventIsValid, func(e any) {
290: 		event, ok := e.(*Event)
291: 		if !ok {
292: 			eb.logger.Error("Could not dump contents of events buffer", logfields.Error, wrongObjTypeErr(e))
293: 			return
294: 		}
295: 		callback(event)
296: 	})
297: }

22: // EndpointKey represents the key value of the endpoints BPF map
23: //
24: // Must be in sync with struct endpoint_key in <bpf/lib/common.h>
25: type EndpointKey struct {
26: 	// represents both IPv6 and IPv4 (in the lowest four bytes)
27: 	IP        types.IPv6 `align:"$union0"`
28: 	Family    uint8      `align:"family"`
29: 	Key       uint8      `align:"key"`
30: 	ClusterID uint16     `align:"cluster_id"`

33: // NewEndpointKey returns an EndpointKey based on the provided IP address. The
34: // address family is automatically detected.
35: func NewEndpointKey(ip net.IP, clusterID uint16) EndpointKey {
36: 	result := EndpointKey{}
37: 
38: 	if ip4 := ip.To4(); ip4 != nil {
39: 		result.Family = EndpointKeyIPv4
40: 		copy(result.IP[:], ip4)
41: 	} else {
42: 		result.Family = EndpointKeyIPv6
43: 		copy(result.IP[:], ip)
44: 	}
45: 	result.Key = 0
46: 	result.ClusterID = clusterID
47: 
48: 	return result
49: }

52: func (k EndpointKey) ToIP() net.IP {
53: 	switch k.Family {
54: 	case EndpointKeyIPv4:
55: 		return k.IP[:4]
56: 	case EndpointKeyIPv6:
57: 		return k.IP[:]
58: 	}
59: 	return nil
60: }

66: 			netipx.MustFromStdIP(ip),
67: 			uint32(k.ClusterID),
68: 		)
69: 		return addrCluster.String() + ":" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", k.Key)
70: 	}

9: 	"errors"

11: 	"log/slog"
12: 	"os"
13: 	"path"
14: 
15: 	"github.com/cilium/ebpf"
16: 	"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
17: 
18: 	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"

20: 	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/spanstat"
21: )

23: // createMap wraps a call to ebpf.NewMapWithOptions while measuring syscall duration.
24: func createMap(spec *ebpf.MapSpec, opts *ebpf.MapOptions) (*ebpf.Map, error) {
25: 	if opts == nil {
26: 		opts = &ebpf.MapOptions{}
27: 	}
28: 
29: 	var duration *spanstat.SpanStat
30: 	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
31: 		duration = spanstat.Start()
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	m, err := ebpf.NewMapWithOptions(spec, *opts)
35: 
36: 	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
37: 		metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.WithLabelValues(metricOpCreate, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Observe(duration.End(err == nil).Total().Seconds())
38: 	}
39: 
40: 	return m, err
41: }
42: 
43: func objCheck(logger *slog.Logger, m *ebpf.Map, path string, mapType ebpf.MapType, keySize, valueSize, maxEntries, flags uint32) bool {
44: 	scopedLogger := logger.With(logfields.Path, path)
45: 	mismatch := false
46: 
47: 	if m.Type() != mapType {
48: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Map type mismatch for BPF map",
49: 			logfields.Old, m.Type(),
50: 			logfields.New, mapType,
51: 		)
52: 		mismatch = true
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	if m.KeySize() != keySize {
56: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Key-size mismatch for BPF map",
57: 			logfields.Old, m.KeySize(),
58: 			logfields.New, keySize,
59: 		)
60: 		mismatch = true
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	if m.ValueSize() != valueSize {
64: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Value-size mismatch for BPF map",
65: 			logfields.Old, m.ValueSize(),
66: 			logfields.New, valueSize,
67: 		)
68: 		mismatch = true
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	if m.MaxEntries() != maxEntries {
72: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Max entries mismatch for BPF map",
73: 			logfields.Old, m.MaxEntries(),
74: 			logfields.New, maxEntries,
75: 		)
76: 		mismatch = true
77: 	}
78: 	if m.Flags() != flags {
79: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Flags mismatch for BPF map",
80: 			logfields.Old, m.Flags(),
81: 			logfields.New, flags,
82: 		)
83: 		mismatch = true
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	if mismatch {
87: 		if m.Type() == ebpf.ProgramArray {
88: 			return false
89: 		}
90: 
91: 		scopedLogger.Warn("Removing map to allow for property upgrade (expect map data loss)")
92: 
93: 		// Kernel still holds map reference count via attached prog.
94: 		// Only exception is prog array, but that is already resolved
95: 		// differently.
96: 		os.Remove(path)
97: 		return true
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	return false
101: }
102: 
103: // OpenOrCreateMap attempts to load the pinned map at "pinDir/<spec.Name>" if
104: // the spec is marked as Pinned. Any parent directories of pinDir are
105: // automatically created. Any pinned maps incompatible with the given spec are
106: // removed and recreated.
107: //
108: // If spec.Pinned is 0, a new Map is always created.
109: func OpenOrCreateMap(logger *slog.Logger, spec *ebpf.MapSpec, pinDir string) (*ebpf.Map, error) {
110: 	var opts ebpf.MapOptions
111: 	if spec.Pinning != 0 {
112: 		if pinDir == "" {
113: 			return nil, errors.New("cannot pin map to empty pinDir")
114: 		}
115: 		if spec.Name == "" {
116: 			return nil, errors.New("cannot load unnamed map from pin")
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		if err := MkdirBPF(pinDir); err != nil {
120: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating map base pinning directory: %w", err)
121: 		}
122: 
123: 		opts.PinPath = pinDir
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	m, err := createMap(spec, &opts)
127: 	if errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrMapIncompatible) {
128: 		// Found incompatible map. Open the pin again to find out why.
129: 		m, err := ebpf.LoadPinnedMap(path.Join(pinDir, spec.Name), nil)
130: 		if err != nil {
131: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open pin of incompatible map: %w", err)
132: 		}
133: 		defer m.Close()
134: 
135: 		logger.Info(
136: 			"Unpinning map with incompatible properties",
137: 			logfields.Path, path.Join(pinDir, spec.Name),
138: 			logfields.Old, []any{
139: 				logfields.Type, m.Type(),
140: 				logfields.KeySize, m.KeySize(),
141: 				logfields.ValueSize, m.ValueSize(),
142: 				logfields.MaxEntries, m.MaxEntries(),
143: 				logfields.Flags, m.Flags(),
144: 			},
145: 			logfields.New, []any{
146: 				logfields.Type, spec.Type,
147: 				logfields.KeySize, spec.KeySize,
148: 				logfields.ValueSize, spec.ValueSize,
149: 				logfields.MaxEntries, spec.MaxEntries,
150: 				logfields.Flags, spec.Flags,
151: 			},
152: 		)
153: 
154: 		// Existing map incompatible with spec. Unpin so it can be recreated.
155: 		if err := m.Unpin(); err != nil {
156: 			return nil, err
157: 		}
158: 
159: 		return createMap(spec, &opts)
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	return m, err
163: }
164: 
165: // GetMtime returns monotonic time that can be used to compare
166: // values with ktime_get_ns() BPF helper, e.g. needed to check
167: // the timeout in sec for BPF entries. We return the raw nsec,
168: // although that is not quite usable for comparison. Go has
169: // runtime.nanotime() but doesn't expose it as API.
170: func GetMtime() (uint64, error) {
171: 	var ts unix.Timespec
172: 
173: 	err := unix.ClockGettime(unix.CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts)
174: 	if err != nil {
175: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("Unable get time: %w", err)
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	return uint64(unix.TimespecToNsec(ts)), nil
179: }

# Cilium eBPF Map API Reference

This document provides a comprehensive reference for the Go user-space API in the `pkg/bpf` package that wraps kernel eBPF maps. It covers map creation, CRUD operations, iteration, pinning, events, and collection loading.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Key/Value Interfaces](#keyvalue-interfaces)
2. [Core Map Type](#core-map-type)
3. [Map Creation Functions](#map-creation-functions)
4. [Map Lifecycle Operations](#map-lifecycle-operations)
5. [CRUD Operations](#crud-operations)
6. [Iteration and Dumping](#iteration-and-dumping)
7. [Batch Operations](#batch-operations)
8. [Pinning and Persistence](#pinning-and-persistence)
9. [Event System](#event-system)
10. [Collection Loading](#collection-loading)
11. [StateDB Reconciler Integration](#statedb-reconciler-integration)
12. [Helper Utilities](#helper-utilities)

---

## Key/Value Interfaces

All keys and values stored in a `Map` must satisfy one of the following interfaces.

### `MapKey`

```go
type MapKey interface {
    fmt.Stringer
    New() MapKey
}
```

`MapKey` is the interface every BPF map key type must implement.

- `String() string` — returns a human-readable representation of the key, used in logging and as the in-memory cache key string.
- `New() MapKey` — allocates and returns a fresh, zeroed pointer to a new instance of the same concrete type. Called internally during iteration to avoid reuse of the same buffer.

### `MapValue`

```go
type MapValue interface {
    fmt.Stringer
    New() MapValue
}
```

`MapValue` is the interface every BPF map value type must implement.

- `String() string` — returns a human-readable representation of the value.
- `New() MapValue` — allocates and returns a fresh, zeroed pointer to a new instance of the same concrete type.

### `MapPerCPUValue`

```go
type MapPerCPUValue interface {
    MapValue
    NewSlice() any
}
```

`MapPerCPUValue` extends `MapValue` for maps of type `PerCPUHash`, `PerCPUArray`, `LRUCPUHash`, or `PerCPUCGroupStorage`. Implement this interface to enable per-CPU value iteration via `DumpPerCPUWithCallback`.

- `NewSlice() any` — allocates and returns a pointer to a slice whose element type is the concrete value struct. The slice is sized to the number of online CPUs.

### Concrete Example: `EndpointKey`

`EndpointKey` is a concrete example of a `MapKey` used in Cilium's endpoint BPF maps. It encodes both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses as a union in the lowest bytes of a 16-byte field.

```go
type EndpointKey struct {
    IP        types.IPv6 // holds both IPv6 and IPv4 (in the lowest 4 bytes)
    Family    uint8      // EndpointKeyIPv4 (1) or EndpointKeyIPv6 (2)
    Key       uint8
    ClusterID uint16
}

func NewEndpointKey(ip net.IP, clusterID uint16) EndpointKey
func (k EndpointKey) ToIP() net.IP
func (k EndpointKey) String() string
```

**Usage:**

```go
key := bpf.NewEndpointKey(net.ParseIP("10.0.0.1"), 0)
fmt.Println(key.String()) // "10.0.0.1:0"
```

---

## Core Map Type

### `Map` Struct

```go
type Map struct {
    Logger *slog.Logger
    // unexported fields
}
```

`Map` is the central type wrapping a kernel eBPF map. It is safe for concurrent use; all exported methods acquire the internal `RWMutex` appropriately.

A `Map` begins life either as a blueprint (holding a `MapSpec`) or as an open handle to an existing kernel map. Once opened or created, the `MapSpec` is consumed and the live kernel handle is retained.

**Key behavioral properties:**

- A `Map` is not open until one of `Open()`, `OpenOrCreate()`, `CreateUnpinned()`, or `Create()` is called (or implicitly by `Lookup`/`Update`/`Delete`/`Dump*`).
- All CRUD methods that acquire the map implicitly call `Open()` if the map is not yet open.
- The `Map` is **not** automatically pinned to the BPF filesystem unless `OpenOrCreate()` or `Create()` is used, or the spec has a non-zero `Pinning` field.

**Builder methods (configure before opening):**

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `WithCache() *Map` | Enables an in-memory value cache. Entries inserted from user-space are stored locally with their desired state and last error. Enables automatic error reconciliation via a background controller. |
| `WithEvents(c option.BPFEventBufferConfig) *Map` | Enables a bounded ring-buffer event log of all map operations (update/delete/delete-all). Required before calling `DumpAndSubscribe`. |
| `WithGroupName(group string) *Map` | Sets the metric group name for capacity/pressure metrics. |
| `WithPressureMetric(registry *metrics.Registry) *Map` | Enables map pressure tracking (fill ratio) with threshold 0 (always reported). |
| `WithPressureMetricThreshold(registry *metrics.Registry, threshold float64) *Map` | Enables map pressure tracking, only reported when pressure exceeds `threshold`. |

**Property accessors (available before and after opening):**

| Method | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `Name` | `() string` | Returns the map's base name. |
| `Path` | `() (string, error)` | Returns the absolute bpffs pin path, computing it from the name if not explicitly set. |
| `Type` | `() ebpf.MapType` | Returns the map type (Hash, Array, LPMTrie, etc.). |
| `KeySize` | `() uint32` | Returns the key size in bytes. |
| `ValueSize` | `() uint32` | Returns the value size in bytes. |
| `MaxEntries` | `() uint32` | Returns the maximum number of entries the map can hold. |
| `Flags` | `() uint32` | Returns the map flags (e.g., `BPF_F_NO_PREALLOC`). |
| `FD` | `() int` | Returns the file descriptor of the open kernel map. Panics if the map is not open. |
| `IsOpen` | `() bool` | Returns true if the map currently has an open kernel handle. |

---

## Map Creation Functions

### `NewMap`

```go
func NewMap(
    name string,
    mapType ebpf.MapType,
    mapKey MapKey,
    mapValue MapValue,
    maxEntries int,
    flags uint32,
) *Map
```

Constructs a new `Map` blueprint. No kernel resources are allocated until one of the lifecycle methods is called.

- `name` — the map's base name; also determines the bpffs pin path (under `tc/globals/` for the Cilium agent).
- `mapKey` / `mapValue` — prototype instances whose concrete type determines the key/value sizes via reflection.
- `flags` — standard BPF map creation flags (e.g., `unix.BPF_F_NO_PREALLOC`).

**Example:**

```go
type MyKey struct {
    Addr uint32
}
func (k *MyKey) String() string { return fmt.Sprintf("%d", k.Addr) }
func (k *MyKey) New() bpf.MapKey { return &MyKey{} }

type MyValue struct {
    Count uint64
}
func (v *MyValue) String() string { return fmt.Sprintf("%d", v.Count) }
func (v *MyValue) New() bpf.MapValue { return &MyValue{} }

m := bpf.NewMap(
    "cilium_my_map",
    ebpf.Hash,
    &MyKey{},
    &MyValue{},
    65536,
    0,
)
```

### `NewMapWithInnerSpec`

```go
func NewMapWithInnerSpec(
    name string,
    mapType ebpf.MapType,
    mapKey MapKey,
    mapValue MapValue,
    maxEntries int,
    flags uint32,
    innerSpec *ebpf.MapSpec,
) *Map
```

Like `NewMap`, but also sets an inner map spec. Used for map-in-map types such as `ebpf.ArrayOfMaps` or `ebpf.HashOfMaps`.

### `OpenMap`

```go
func OpenMap(pinPath string, key MapKey, value MapValue) (*Map, error)
```

Opens an **already-pinned** BPF map from the BPF filesystem.

- `pinPath` must be an absolute path.
- The map is opened but **not** re-pinned; the pin already exists.
- Returns the open `Map` or an error if the pin does not exist or is inaccessible.

**Example:**

```go
m, err := bpf.OpenMap("/sys/fs/bpf/tc/globals/cilium_my_map", &MyKey{}, &MyValue{})
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer m.Close()
```

---

## Map Lifecycle Operations

### `OpenOrCreate`

```go
func (m *Map) OpenOrCreate() error
```

The primary lifecycle method for maps that should persist on the BPF filesystem.

**Behavior:**

1. If the map is already open, returns `nil` immediately.
2. Attempts to load an existing pinned map at `m.path` (which defaults to `MapPath(m.name)`).
3. If found, validates that the existing map's type, key size, value size, max entries, and flags match the spec. If they do not match and the map is not a program array, the pin is deleted and a new map is created in its place (data loss warning: previous entries are lost).
4. If not found, creates a new map and pins it at `m.path`.
5. The `MapSpec` is consumed after a successful open/create, setting the internal spec field to `nil`.

**Pinning:** Maps opened with `OpenOrCreate` are always pinned. The pin is created at `MapPath(m.name)` under the BPF filesystem root.

### `Create`

```go
func (m *Map) Create() error
```

Equivalent to calling `OpenOrCreate()` followed immediately by `Close()`. Useful when you need the map to exist on the filesystem (e.g., to be loaded by the kernel datapath) but do not need an open file descriptor in the current process after creation.

**Note:** Unlike `CreateUnpinned`, this method **does** pin the map.

### `CreateUnpinned`

```go
func (m *Map) CreateUnpinned() error
```

Creates the map in the kernel **without** pinning it to the BPF filesystem. The map exists only as long as there is at least one open file descriptor referencing it.

Use this for transient maps used exclusively in the current process (e.g., test fixtures, ephemeral communication maps).

### `Open`

```go
func (m *Map) Open() error
```

Opens an existing pinned map by loading it from the BPF filesystem path. Does not create the map if it does not exist. Returns an error if the pin is not found.

Most CRUD methods call `Open()` implicitly when the map is not yet open.

### `Recreate`

```go
func (m *Map) Recreate() error
```

Removes any existing pin at the map's pin path and then creates and pins a fresh map. Returns an error if the map is already open (i.e., has an open kernel handle). Use when you need a guaranteed-clean slate, discarding all previous entries.

### `Close`

```go
func (m *Map) Close() error
```

Closes the kernel file descriptor. If `WithCache` was enabled, the background error resolver controller is also stopped. After `Close()`, the map can be reopened with `Open()` or `OpenOrCreate()`.

### `IsOpen`

```go
func (m *Map) IsOpen() bool
```

Returns `true` if the map currently holds an open kernel file descriptor.

## CRUD Operations

### `Lookup`

```go
func (m *Map) Lookup(key MapKey) (MapValue, error)
```

Looks up a single entry in the map. Opens the map implicitly if not already open.

- Returns the value (a newly allocated `MapValue` instance) on success.
- Returns `nil, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist` if the key is not present.

**Example:**

```go
if err := m.OpenOrCreate(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
val, err := m.Lookup(&MyKey{Addr: 0x0a000001})
if errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) {
    fmt.Println("not found")
} else if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
} else {
    fmt.Println(val.(*MyValue).Count)
}
```

### `Update`

```go
func (m *Map) Update(key MapKey, value MapValue) error
```

Inserts or updates an entry. Opens the map implicitly if not already open.

- Semantics: `BPF_MAP_UPDATE_ELEM` with flag `BPF_ANY` (insert or update).
- If `WithCache` is enabled, the entry is mirrored in the in-memory cache. If the kernel update fails, the cache entry is marked `Insert` (desired) with the error recorded, and a background error resolver is scheduled.
- If `WithEvents` is enabled, a `MapUpdate` event is emitted to the event buffer.

### `Delete`

```go
func (m *Map) Delete(key MapKey) error
```

Deletes the entry for the given key. Returns an error if the key does not exist.

- If `WithCache` is enabled and deletion fails, the cache entry is marked `Delete` (desired) and the background error resolver is scheduled.
- If `WithEvents` is enabled, a `MapDelete` event is emitted.

### `SilentDelete`

```go
func (m *Map) SilentDelete(key MapKey) (deleted bool, err error)
```

Deletes the entry for the given key. If the key is not present, returns `(false, nil)` instead of an error. The `deleted` return value indicates whether the key actually existed.

**Use when** missing entries are expected and you do not want spurious error metric increments.

### `DeleteLocked`

```go
func (m *Map) DeleteLocked(key MapKey) error
```

Deletes the entry for the given key, **assuming the map's internal lock is already held by the caller**.

**Critical:** This method must only be called from within a `DumpCallback` (i.e., from within `DumpReliablyWithCallback`). Calling `Delete` from inside a `DumpCallback` would deadlock because the dump holds the lock for writing; `DeleteLocked` bypasses the lock acquisition, enabling safe deletion while iterating.

```go
// Correct: delete inside DumpReliablyWithCallback using DeleteLocked
stats := bpf.NewDumpStats(m)
err := m.DumpReliablyWithCallback(func(key bpf.MapKey, value bpf.MapValue) {
    if shouldDelete(key, value) {
        _ = m.DeleteLocked(key)
    }
}, stats)
```

### `DeleteAll`

```go
func (m *Map) DeleteAll() error
```

Deletes all entries by iterating the map and deleting each entry individually. Opens the map implicitly if not already open.

**Concurrency note:** Entries added concurrently while deletion is in progress may not be deleted (the iteration cursor may not reach them).

If `WithCache` is enabled, all cache entries are marked `Delete` prior to iteration; successfully deleted entries are removed from the cache, and failures are tracked.

If `WithEvents` is enabled, a single `MapDeleteAll` event is emitted after iteration completes.

### `ClearAll`

```go
func (m *Map) ClearAll() error
```

Zeroes out the values of all entries without deleting the keys. Useful for maps where keys are fixed (e.g., per-CPU arrays). Returns an error if `WithEvents` or `WithCache` is enabled (these options are unsupported for `ClearAll`).

## Iteration and Dumping

### `DumpCallback`

```go
type DumpCallback func(key MapKey, value MapValue)
```

Called once per map entry during iteration. The `key` and `value` are fresh allocations for each invocation; callers may retain them safely.

**Important:** Do not call `Delete()` (or any map method that acquires the lock) from inside a `DumpCallback` used with `DumpReliablyWithCallback`. Use `DeleteLocked` instead.

### `DumpWithCallback`

```go
func (m *Map) DumpWithCallback(cb DumpCallback) error
```

Iterates all entries and invokes `cb` for each one. Not resilient to concurrent map modifications; if an entry is removed during iteration, the iterator may skip entries or restart.

For maps that may be modified during iteration, prefer `DumpReliablyWithCallback`.

### `DumpWithCallbackIfExists`

```go
func (m *Map) DumpWithCallbackIfExists(cb DumpCallback) error
```

Like `DumpWithCallback`, but returns `nil` immediately if the map's pin file does not exist, rather than returning an error.

### `DumpReliablyWithCallback`

```go
func (m *Map) DumpReliablyWithCallback(cb DumpCallback, stats *DumpStats) error
```

An iteration method that is resilient to concurrent modifications. It tracks the previously seen key so that if the current key is deleted mid-iteration, it can restart from the last valid key rather than from the beginning of the map.

**Parameters:**

- `cb` — called for each valid entry.
- `stats` — a `*DumpStats` initialized via `NewDumpStats(m)`; must not be nil.

**Termination conditions:**

- Returns `nil` when all entries have been visited (`stats.Completed == true`).
- Returns `ErrMaxLookup` if the number of iterations exceeds `4 × MaxEntries` (guard against infinite loops on a continuously mutated map).
- Returns any other kernel-level error encountered.

**Lock behavior:** Holds the write lock for the entire duration. This allows `DeleteLocked` to be called safely from within the callback.

**Example:**

```go
stats := bpf.NewDumpStats(m)
err := m.DumpReliablyWithCallback(func(key bpf.MapKey, value bpf.MapValue) {
    k := key.(*MyKey)
    v := value.(*MyValue)
    fmt.Printf("%d -> %d\n", k.Addr, v.Count)
}, stats)
if err != nil {
    log.Printf("dump error: %v (lookups: %d, interrupted: %d)", err, stats.Lookup, stats.Interrupted)
}
```

### `DumpPerCPUWithCallback`

```go
type DumpPerCPUCallback func(key MapKey, values any)

func (m *Map) DumpPerCPUWithCallback(cb DumpPerCPUCallback) error
```

Iterates a per-CPU map and calls `cb` with the key and the full per-CPU slice of values for each entry. The value type must implement `MapPerCPUValue`. The `values` argument passed to the callback is a pointer to a slice of the concrete value struct, one element per CPU.

### `Dump`

```go
func (m *Map) Dump(hash map[string][]string) error
```

Dumps the entire map into the provided `map[string][]string`, where the key is the string representation of the map key and the values are string representations of the map value(s). Suitable for simple debugging and serialization.

### `DumpIfExists`

```go
func (m *Map) DumpIfExists(hash map[string][]string) error
```

Like `Dump`, but returns `nil` immediately if the map pin does not exist.

### `DumpStats`

```go
type DumpStats struct {
    Started            time.Time
    Finished           time.Time
    Lookup             uint32
    LookupFailed       uint32
    PrevKeyUnavailable uint32
    KeyFallback        uint32
    MaxEntries         uint32
    Interrupted        uint32
    Completed          bool
}

func NewDumpStats(m *Map) *DumpStats
func (d *DumpStats) Duration() time.Duration
```

`DumpStats` is populated by `DumpReliablyWithCallback` to track iteration quality. Fields of note:

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `Lookup` | Total number of `NextKey` calls performed. |
| `LookupFailed` | Number of lookups where the key could not be found in the map (entry was deleted mid-iteration). |
| `KeyFallback` | Number of times iteration fell back to the previous key because the current key was deleted. |
| `Interrupted` | Number of times iteration restarted from the beginning of the map after key loss with no previous key available. |
| `Completed` | `true` if iteration completed normally without hitting `ErrMaxLookup`. |

## Batch Operations

Batch operations use the kernel's `BPF_MAP_LOOKUP_BATCH` syscall for higher throughput than single-entry lookup. They are supported only for `Hash`, `LRUHash`, and `LPMTrie` map types.

### `BatchLookup`

```go
func (m *Map) BatchLookup(
    cursor *ebpf.MapBatchCursor,
    keysOut, valuesOut any,
    opts *ebpf.BatchOptions,
) (int, error)
```

Performs a single batch lookup starting from `cursor`. Returns the number of entries read. When the map is exhausted, returns `(n, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist)`.

### `BatchCount`

```go
func (m *Map) BatchCount() (count int, err error)
```

Returns the total number of entries in the map using repeated batch lookups. Only supported for `Hash`, `LRUHash`, and `LPMTrie` maps. Internally handles `ENOSPC` retries by doubling the chunk size.

### `BatchIterator`

`BatchIterator` is a typed, generic wrapper that provides ergonomic iteration via Go's range-over-function (iter.Seq2) interface.

```go
type BatchIterator[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] struct { ... }

func NewBatchIterator[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](m *Map) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]
```

**Type parameters:**

- `KT` — the concrete key struct type (e.g., `MyKey`).
- `VT` — the concrete value struct type (e.g., `MyValue`).
- `KP` — a pointer-to-KT that also satisfies `MapKey` (e.g., `*MyKey`).
- `VP` — a pointer-to-VT that also satisfies `MapValue` (e.g., `*MyValue`).

**Important:** Each call to `IterateAll` resets all internal state, including the error. If you reuse an iterator across multiple dumps, check `Err()` before the next `IterateAll` call returns.

#### `IterateAll`

```go
func (bi *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) IterateAll(
    ctx context.Context,
    opts ...BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP],
) iter.Seq2[KP, VP]
```

Returns an `iter.Seq2` that yields `(*KT, *VT)` pairs. Compatible with `for k, v := range iter.IterateAll(ctx) { ... }`.

**ENOSPC retry behavior:** If the kernel returns `ENOSPC` (the allocated batch buffer was too small for the hash bucket), `BatchIterator` automatically doubles the buffer size and retries from the same cursor position. By default, up to 3 retries are attempted. If all retries are exhausted, the error is recorded in `Err()`.

**Starting chunk size:** The initial buffer size is automatically computed as the nearest power of two greater than or equal to `√(2 × MaxEntries)`. This approximates the expected number of entries before the first hash collision, minimizing the likelihood of `ENOSPC`.

#### `Err`

```go
func (bi BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) Err() error
```

Returns the first error encountered during the previous call to `IterateAll`. Returns `nil` if iteration completed successfully.

#### Iterator Options

Options are passed to `IterateAll` as variadic arguments:

| Option Function | Description |
|----------------|-------------|
| `WithMaxRetries[KT, VT, KP, VP](retries uint32)` | Overrides the default 3 ENOSPC retry limit. |
| `WithStartingChunkSize[KT, VT, KP, VP](size int)` | Overrides the automatically computed starting batch buffer size. |
| `WithEBPFBatchOpts[KT, VT, KP, VP](opts *ebpf.BatchOptions)` | Passes low-level batch options to the underlying syscall. |

#### `CountAll`

```go
func CountAll[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](
    ctx context.Context,
    iter *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP],
) (int, error)
```

Convenience function that drives a `BatchIterator` to completion and returns the total entry count.

**Full Example:**

```go
// MyKey implements MapKey, *MyKey implements KeyPointer[MyKey].
// MyValue implements MapValue, *MyValue implements ValuePointer[MyValue].

iter := bpf.NewBatchIterator[MyKey, MyValue, *MyKey, *MyValue](m)

for k, v := range iter.IterateAll(ctx) {
    fmt.Printf("key=%d value=%d\n", k.Addr, v.Count)
}

if err := iter.Err(); err != nil {
    log.Printf("iteration error: %v", err)
}
```

## Pinning and Persistence

BPF maps are pinned to the BPF filesystem (bpffs) to persist beyond the lifetime of the creating process. The Cilium agent mounts bpffs and uses it to share maps between the agent and the kernel datapath programs.

### BPF Filesystem Paths

```go
func BPFFSRoot() string
func CiliumPath() string
func TCGlobalsPath() string
func MapPath(logger *slog.Logger, name string) string
func LocalMapName(name string, id uint16) string
func LocalMapPath(logger *slog.Logger, name string, id uint16) string
```

| Function | Returns |
|----------|---------|
| `BPFFSRoot()` | The root of the BPF filesystem (default: `/sys/fs/bpf`). |
| `CiliumPath()` | `<BPFFSRoot>/cilium` — the Cilium-specific directory. |
| `TCGlobalsPath()` | `<BPFFSRoot>/tc/globals` — the legacy map pin path used by the agent for per-node global maps. |
| `MapPath(logger, name)` | The full path where a named map would be pinned; equivalent to `<TCGlobalsPath>/<name>` when running as the Cilium agent. |
| `LocalMapName(name, id)` | Formats a map name with a 5-digit endpoint ID suffix: `<name><id>`. |
| `LocalMapPath(logger, name, id)` | Equivalent to `MapPath(logger, LocalMapName(name, id))`. |

**Note:** `BPFFSRoot()` and related functions lock down the root path on first access. Attempting to change the root after the first read panics.

### `CheckOrMountFS`

```go
func CheckOrMountFS(logger *slog.Logger, bpfRoot string)
```

Ensures the BPF filesystem is mounted at `bpfRoot` (or at the default locations `/sys/fs/bpf` / `/run/cilium/bpffs` if `bpfRoot` is empty). Logs a warning and mounts automatically if not already mounted. This is called once during agent startup.

### `MkdirBPF`

```go
func MkdirBPF(path string) error
```

Creates a directory on the BPF filesystem with the correct permissions (0755). Use this instead of `os.MkdirAll` for bpffs paths.

### Pinning Methods on `Map`

#### `Unpin`

```go
func (m *Map) Unpin() error
```

Removes the map's pin file from the BPF filesystem using `os.RemoveAll`. The map's kernel object remains alive as long as there is at least one open file descriptor.

#### `UnpinIfExists`

```go
func (m *Map) UnpinIfExists() error
```

Like `Unpin`, but does nothing and returns `nil` if the pin file does not exist.

**Pin vs. unpin semantics:**

- Pinning a map to bpffs keeps it alive after the pinning process exits. The kernel holds a reference as long as the pin file exists or any file descriptor is open.
- Unpinning removes the filesystem reference, but the map continues to exist until all file descriptors are closed.
- `OpenOrCreate` always pins. `CreateUnpinned` never pins.
- Calling `Recreate()` removes the existing pin, then creates and re-pins a fresh map.

## Event System

The event system provides an audit log of map mutations and supports live streaming of events to subscribers. It must be explicitly enabled with `WithEvents`.

### Enabling Events

```go
m := bpf.NewMap("cilium_my_map", ebpf.Hash, &MyKey{}, &MyValue{}, 1024, 0).
    WithEvents(option.BPFEventBufferConfig{
        Enabled: true,
        MaxSize: 4096,
        TTL:     5 * time.Minute,
    })
```

- `MaxSize` — maximum number of events held in the ring buffer. When the buffer is full, the oldest entry is overwritten.
- `TTL` — if non-zero, a background controller runs at `TTL` intervals and evicts events older than the TTL. Set to zero to retain events indefinitely (bounded only by `MaxSize`).

### Event Types

```go
type Action uint8

const (
    MapUpdate    Action = iota // emitted on Update()
    MapDelete                  // emitted on Delete() / SilentDelete() / DeleteLocked()
    MapDeleteAll               // emitted once on DeleteAll() (aggregate event)
)

func (e Action) String() string
```

### `Event`

```go
type Event struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    // unexported: action Action, cacheEntry (Key, Value, DesiredAction, LastError)
}

func (e *Event) GetAction() string
func (e Event) GetKey() string
func (e Event) GetValue() string
func (e Event) GetLastError() error
func (e Event) GetDesiredAction() DesiredAction
```

- `GetAction()` — returns `"update"`, `"delete"`, or `"delete-all"`.
- `GetKey()` — returns the string representation of the key (`"<nil>"` for `MapDeleteAll`).
- `GetValue()` — returns the string representation of the value (`"<nil>"` for deletes).
- `GetLastError()` — returns any error from the originating operation.
- `GetDesiredAction()` — returns the desired cache state at event time (`OK`, `Insert`, or `Delete`).

### `EventCallbackFunc`

```go
type EventCallbackFunc func(*Event)
```

A callback invoked once per historical event during a dump of the event buffer.

### `DumpAndSubscribe`

```go
func (m *Map) DumpAndSubscribe(callback EventCallbackFunc, follow bool) (*Handle, error)
```

Atomically dumps existing events from the ring buffer and optionally establishes a live subscription. Returns a `*Handle` if `follow == true`.

- `callback` — if non-nil, called synchronously for each existing event in the ring buffer (respecting the TTL filter).
- `follow` — if `true`, returns a `*Handle` whose channel receives future events. If `false`, returns `nil, nil` after the dump.

The dump and subscription setup are performed under the map's read lock, preventing any events from being missed between the historical dump and the live subscription.

**Maximum concurrent subscriptions:** 32. Attempting to exceed this limit returns an error.

**Example:**

```go
handle, err := m.DumpAndSubscribe(func(e *bpf.Event) {
    fmt.Printf("historical: %s key=%s\n", e.GetAction(), e.GetKey())
}, true)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer handle.Close()

for event := range handle.C() {
    fmt.Printf("live: %s key=%s value=%s\n", event.GetAction(), event.GetKey(), event.GetValue())
}

// handle.C() is closed when Close() is called or when the subscriber falls behind.
```

### `Handle`

```go
type Handle struct { ... }

func (h *Handle) C() <-chan *Event
func (h *Handle) Close()
```

`Handle` is the live event subscription object.

- `C()` — returns a read-only channel of `*Event`. Events are buffered (channel capacity: 32). The channel is closed when `Close()` is called or when the map's event system forcibly drops the subscription.
- `Close()` — signals that the subscriber is done. Safe to call multiple times.

**Back-pressure:** The event system is non-blocking. If a subscriber's channel buffer is full when a new event arrives, the subscription is forcibly closed with an error. Subscribers must read events promptly to avoid being dropped.

### `IsEventsEnabled`

```go
func (m *Map) IsEventsEnabled() bool
```

Returns `true` if the events buffer was enabled via `WithEvents`.

## Collection Loading

Collection loading functions wrap `cilium/ebpf`'s collection API with Cilium-specific behaviors such as tail call resolution, map renaming, constant injection, and automatic pinning.

### `CollectionOptions`

```go
type CollectionOptions struct {
    ebpf.CollectionOptions

    // Constants holds a pointer to a struct from pkg/datapath/config.
    // Fields of the struct override BPF runtime configurables declared with DECLARE_CONFIG.
    Constants any

    // MapRenames maps CollectionSpec map keys to new names applied before loading.
    MapRenames map[string]string
}
```

### `LoadCollectionSpec`

```go
func LoadCollectionSpec(logger *slog.Logger, path string) (*ebpf.CollectionSpec, error)
```

Loads and parses a BPF ELF object file into a `CollectionSpec`. In addition to standard ELF parsing, this function:

1. Validates that all programs have a known type (annotated with `__section_entry` or `__declare_tail()`).
2. Performs static reachability analysis to identify which tail call programs are reachable from the entry points.
3. Removes unreachable tail call programs from the spec to avoid loading dead code.
4. Populates the `cilium_calls` program array map with the reachable tail call programs.

### `LoadCollection`

```go
func LoadCollection(
    logger *slog.Logger,
    spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec,
    opts *CollectionOptions,
) (*ebpf.Collection, func() error, error)
```

Loads the collection spec into the kernel. Returns:

- `*ebpf.Collection` — the live collection containing all loaded programs and maps.
- `func() error` — a **commit function** that must be called after all entry-point programs from the collection have been attached to their kernel hooks. The commit replaces map pins on the bpffs for incompatible or `CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE`-marked maps.
- `error` — loading error, possibly a `*ebpf.VerifierError`.

**The commit function is critical.** For programs sharing a tail calls map: attach all entry-points first, then call commit once. Committing too early can leave the tail calls map in an intermediate state, causing some programs to invoke stale tail call targets.

**Map pinning behavior during `LoadCollection`:**

- Maps with `LIBBPF_PIN_BY_NAME` in the spec are automatically loaded from `opts.Maps.PinPath/<name>` if the pin exists and is compatible.
- If a pinned map is incompatible with the spec, the pin is removed and a fresh map is created. The new pin is committed by the returned commit function.
- Maps marked `CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE` (`PinReplace`) are always replaced: they are created fresh and their pins are updated by the commit function.

**Example:**

```go
spec, err := bpf.LoadCollectionSpec(logger, "/path/to/bpf_prog.o")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

coll, commit, err := bpf.LoadCollection(logger, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{
    CollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{
        Maps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.CiliumPath()},
    },
    Constants: &myDatapathConfig,
    MapRenames: map[string]string{"cilium_calls_00000": "cilium_calls_00042"},
})
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Attach programs to hooks...
link, err := link.XDP(iface, coll.Programs["cil_xdp_entry"], nil)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Only commit after all entry-points are attached.
if err := commit(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
```

### `LoadAndAssign`

```go
func LoadAndAssign(
    logger *slog.Logger,
    to any,
    spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec,
    opts *CollectionOptions,
) (func() error, error)
```

A convenience wrapper around `LoadCollection` that additionally calls `coll.Assign(to)` to populate a struct with the collection's programs and maps. The struct fields are matched by name to the collection's programs and maps.

- Returns the commit function (same semantics as `LoadCollection`).
- On verifier error, writes the verifier log to `stderr`.
- Wraps `LoadCollection` and `coll.Assign` errors.

**Example:**

```go
var objs struct {
    CilXdpEntry *ebpf.Program `ebpf:"cil_xdp_entry"`
    CiliumCalls *ebpf.Map     `ebpf:"cilium_calls"`
}

commit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(logger, &objs, spec, opts)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer objs.CilXdpEntry.Close()
defer objs.CiliumCalls.Close()

// Attach objs.CilXdpEntry ...

if err := commit(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
```

## StateDB Reconciler Integration

The `pkg/bpf` package provides integration with Cilium's `statedb` reconciler for declarative BPF map management.

### `KeyValue` Interface

```go
type KeyValue interface {
    BinaryKey() encoding.BinaryMarshaler
    BinaryValue() encoding.BinaryMarshaler
}
```

Objects stored in a StateDB table that are backed by a BPF map must implement `KeyValue`. The marshaled binary forms are used directly in BPF map operations.

### `StructBinaryMarshaler`

```go
type StructBinaryMarshaler struct {
    Target any // pointer to struct
}

func (m StructBinaryMarshaler) MarshalBinary() ([]byte, error)
```

A convenience `BinaryMarshaler` for structs composed of primitive fields. Copies the struct's memory directly as bytes, matching the layout expected by the kernel. Use this in `BinaryKey()` and `BinaryValue()` implementations when the struct is already in the correct wire format.

```go
func (x *MyEntry) BinaryKey() encoding.BinaryMarshaler {
    return bpf.StructBinaryMarshaler{&x.Key}
}
func (x *MyEntry) BinaryValue() encoding.BinaryMarshaler {
    return bpf.StructBinaryMarshaler{&x.Value}
}
```

### `NewMapOps`

```go
func NewMapOps[KV KeyValue](m *Map) reconciler.Operations[KV]
```

Returns a `reconciler.Operations[KV]` implementation backed by a `*Map`. This implements `Update`, `Delete`, and `Prune` operations suitable for use with the StateDB reconciler.

- `Update` — calls `m.m.Put(kv.BinaryKey(), kv.BinaryValue())`.
- `Delete` — calls `m.m.Delete(kv.BinaryKey())`; silently ignores missing keys.
- `Prune` — iterates the map and deletes any entry whose key is not present in the desired state table.

`ErrMapNotOpened` is returned by all operations if the map does not have an open kernel handle.

## Helper Utilities

### `MapOut` (Dependency Injection)

```go
type BpfMap any

type MapOut[T any] struct {
    cell.Out
    Map    T
    BpfMap BpfMap `group:"bpf-maps"`
}

func NewMapOut[T any](m T) MapOut[T]
```

`MapOut` is a Hive/DI helper that registers a map into the `bpf-maps` value group, ensuring all maps are created before the datapath loader is invoked.

**Usage in a Hive cell:**

```go
cell.Provide(func() MapOut[*MyMap] {
    return bpf.NewMapOut(newMyMap())
})
```

### `GetMtime`

```go
func GetMtime() (uint64, error)
```

Returns the current monotonic clock time in nanoseconds, matching the value returned by the `ktime_get_ns()` BPF helper. Used to compare against timeout fields in BPF map entries.

### `OpenOrCreateMap` (low-level)

```go
func OpenOrCreateMap(logger *slog.Logger, spec *ebpf.MapSpec, pinDir string) (*ebpf.Map, error)
```

Lower-level function that attempts to open a pinned map or create a new one. If the existing pin is incompatible, it is removed and the map is recreated. This underlies `Map.OpenOrCreate()` but operates directly on `ebpf.MapSpec` and `ebpf.Map`.

### `LocalMapName` and `LocalMapPath`

```go
func LocalMapName(name string, id uint16) string
func LocalMapPath(logger *slog.Logger, name string, id uint16) string
```

Generate endpoint-scoped map names and paths. The `id` (endpoint ID) is zero-padded to 5 digits and appended to `name`.

```go
name := bpf.LocalMapName("cilium_ct4_", 42) // "cilium_ct4_00042"
path := bpf.LocalMapPath(logger, "cilium_ct4_", 42) // "/sys/fs/bpf/tc/globals/cilium_ct4_00042"
```

---

## Common Usage Patterns

### Basic Map CRUD

```go
// 1. Define key and value types.
type RouteKey struct{ Prefix uint32 }
func (k *RouteKey) String() string { return fmt.Sprintf("%d", k.Prefix) }
func (k *RouteKey) New() bpf.MapKey { return &RouteKey{} }

type RouteValue struct{ NextHop uint32 }
func (v *RouteValue) String() string { return fmt.Sprintf("%d", v.NextHop) }
func (v *RouteValue) New() bpf.MapValue { return &RouteValue{} }

// 2. Create and open the map.
m := bpf.NewMap("cilium_routes", ebpf.Hash, &RouteKey{}, &RouteValue{}, 1024, 0)
if err := m.OpenOrCreate(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer m.Close()

// 3. Insert an entry.
if err := m.Update(&RouteKey{Prefix: 0x0a000000}, &RouteValue{NextHop: 0xc0a80001}); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// 4. Look up an entry.
val, err := m.Lookup(&RouteKey{Prefix: 0x0a000000})
if err != nil {
    log.Printf("lookup: %v", err)
} else {
    fmt.Printf("next hop: %d\n", val.(*RouteValue).NextHop)
}

// 5. Delete an entry.
if err := m.Delete(&RouteKey{Prefix: 0x0a000000}); err != nil {
    log.Printf("delete: %v", err)
}
```

### Iteration with `DumpReliablyWithCallback` and In-Loop Deletion

```go
stats := bpf.NewDumpStats(m)
err := m.DumpReliablyWithCallback(func(key bpf.MapKey, value bpf.MapValue) {
    k := key.(*RouteKey)
    if isStale(k) {
        // Must use DeleteLocked inside DumpReliablyWithCallback.
        if delErr := m.DeleteLocked(key); delErr != nil {
            log.Printf("delete: %v", delErr)
        }
    }
}, stats)
if err != nil {
    log.Printf("dump: %v (completed=%v)", err, stats.Completed)
}
```

### Batch Iteration

```go
iter := bpf.NewBatchIterator[RouteKey, RouteValue, *RouteKey, *RouteValue](m)

for k, v := range iter.IterateAll(context.Background()) {
    fmt.Printf("%d -> %d\n", k.Prefix, v.NextHop)
}

if err := iter.Err(); err != nil {
    log.Printf("batch iteration error: %v", err)
}
```

### Event Subscription and Cleanup

```go
m := bpf.NewMap("cilium_routes", ebpf.Hash, &RouteKey{}, &RouteValue{}, 1024, 0).
    WithEvents(option.BPFEventBufferConfig{
        Enabled: true,
        MaxSize: 1000,
        TTL:     10 * time.Minute,
    })

if err := m.OpenOrCreate(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer m.Close()

// Dump historical events and subscribe to future ones.
handle, err := m.DumpAndSubscribe(func(e *bpf.Event) {
    fmt.Printf("[historical] %s %s\n", e.GetAction(), e.GetKey())
}, true)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer handle.Close()

ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
defer cancel()

go func() {
    for {
        select {
        case event, ok := <-handle.C():
            if !ok {
                // Channel closed: subscription was dropped or Close() was called.
                return
            }
            fmt.Printf("[live] %s %s -> %s\n", event.GetAction(), event.GetKey(), event.GetValue())
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        }
    }
}()

// Perform operations...
_ = m.Update(&RouteKey{Prefix: 1}, &RouteValue{NextHop: 2})
_ = m.Delete(&RouteKey{Prefix: 1})
```

### Pinning and Unpinning

```go
// Create and pin the map.
m := bpf.NewMap("cilium_routes", ebpf.Hash, &RouteKey{}, &RouteValue{}, 1024, 0)
if err := m.OpenOrCreate(); err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
m.Close() // Pin persists; map is accessible by path.

// Later, open the map by its pin path.
m2, err := bpf.OpenMap(bpf.MapPath(logger, "cilium_routes"), &RouteKey{}, &RouteValue{})
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer m2.Close()

// When the map is no longer needed, remove its pin.
if err := m2.Unpin(); err != nil {
    log.Printf("unpin: %v", err)
}
// m2 kernel object still alive until m2.Close() is called.
m2.Close()
// Now the kernel object is fully released.
```
